structure.description: Embed aggregate rating (stars + count) from FK-referenced entity inline in each catalog card as clickable chip opening target detail, making reputation a first-class scan-time signal.

rationale.hypothesis: Decisions in transactional catalogs (jobs, sellers, venues) depend on reputation of the related actor, not just the item itself. Surfacing aggregate rating at card level — before detail drill-in — shortens the trust-evaluation loop without inflating the card into a dashboard.

evidence.description: Company rating (★4.4 · 4 reviews) embedded under company name in every job card; tap opens company profile, not job detail
Same pattern: employer rating on job card
Host rating shown on listing card before detail

counterexample.description: Product card shows rating of product itself, not of seller — when FK target is not the trust-bearing entity, embedding its rating adds noise

shouldMatch.reason: Listing→seller with seller rating is prime use case
Specialist cards benefit from inline rating summary

shouldNotMatch.reason: Poll FK to creator — creator rating is not a decision signal
No rating semantics on workflow authors
